economic_notes: Adams Lake Development Corporation fully consolidated. Sexqeltkemc Enterprises Inc. and Sexqeltkemc Limited Partnership accounted for by modified equity method. Timber harvesting and logging operations active. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing Limited Partnership investment. Forest & Range Consultation and Revenue Sharing Agreement funds restricted. COVID-19 noted in auditor's report as a subsequent event with uncertain impact.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Auditor is Daley LLP of Kamloops BC. Adams Lake Development Corporation is fully consolidated subsidiary. Sexqeltkemc Enterprises Inc. and Sexqeltkemc Limited Partnership accounted for by modified equity method. Secwepemc RFA and Sexqeltkemc Lakes Division proportionately consolidated (both at zero for 2020). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $6,383,007 + CMHC $125,861 + Province of BC $3,345,448 + First Nations Health Authority $1,502,400 = $11,356,716. OSR = $22,158,911 - $11,356,716 = $10,802,195. Demand loan of $93,652 also exists (term loan). Segmented schedule (Appendix 2) was partially cut off in source text so segment-level expense breakdown not fully extractable. COVID-19 noted in auditor report as a subsequent event. Large timber harvesting revenue of $3,335,206 in 2020 vs $1,358,125 in 2019. Significant increase in capital asset acquisitions ($4,899,731 vs $736,674 prior year). Net financial assets per statement = $14,333,367.
